# Kumaraguru College of Technology
## History and Founding
### Founder and Vision
Arutchelvar Dr. N. Mahalingam, born on March 21, 1923, in Pollachi, Tamil Nadu, to P. Nachimuthu Gounder, was a renowned philanthropist, industrialist, and educationist who chaired the Sakthi Group, expanding it from a family business he joined in 1943 into a ₹6,000 crore conglomerate involved in sugar, automobiles, finance, distilleries, power, and logistics. In 1984, he founded Kumaraguru College of Technology under the auspices of the Ramanandha Adigalar Foundation to advance technical education in response to industrial demands. Mahalingam passed away on October 2, 2014, leaving a profound impact on education and industry in Tamil Nadu.
Mahalingam's vision for the college centered on holistic education that integrates technical expertise with ethical and moral values, drawing inspiration from Gandhian principles of simplicity and service as well as the practical needs of industry. He aimed to cultivate "engineers with a human touch," emphasizing human resource development and institutional growth to produce professionals who contribute meaningfully to society beyond mere technical proficiency. This mission reflected his commitment to value-based learning, aligning with his broader philosophy of uplifting communities through education and ethical industrial practices.
In recognition of his extensive contributions to trade, industry, and philanthropy, Mahalingam was awarded the Padma Bhushan by the Government of India in 2007. Mahalingam's legacy endures through annual commemorations of his birth on March 21 and remembrance on October 2, as well as initiatives like the Mahatma Gandhi Merit Scholarship, which he initiated to reward academic excellence and perseverance among students

# Kumaraguru College of Technology
## History and Founding
### Establishment
Kumaraguru College of Technology was established in 1984 under the Ramanandha Adigalar Foundation, a charitable educational trust of the Sakthi Group, with a vision to provide quality engineering education in Coimbatore. The institution began as a private self-financing engineering college affiliated to Anna University, starting with undergraduate programs in core engineering disciplines such as Civil, Mechanical, Electrical and Electronics, and Electronics and Communication Engineering and basic infrastructure on a modest campus.
Over the subsequent decades, the college experienced significant growth, with its campus on a 156-acre site in Coimbatore's IT corridor to accommodate increasing academic and research needs. Student enrollment, which commenced with small initial batches in the 1980s, has since grown to over 6,000 students across various programs. Postgraduate programs were introduced in the post-1990s era, including the Master of Computer Applications in 1993, followed by additional M.E. and M.Tech. offerings, while Ph.D. programs began in fields like biotechnology in 2002.
Key milestones include the conferment of autonomous status by the University Grants Commission in 2007, enabling curriculum flexibility and industry-aligned updates. The National Assessment and Accreditation Council (NAAC) granted five-year accreditation in the first cycle, an A grade with a CGPA of 3.21 in the second cycle valid until 2021, and an A++ grade with a CGPA of 3.62 in the third cycle in 2022. The college has introduced emerging programs such as B.Tech. in Artificial Intelligence and Data Science to address technological advancements

It remains autonomous as of 2025. The college offers 14 undergraduate programs (B.E. and B.Tech.), 15 postgraduate programs (M.E., M.Tech., M.C.A., and M.B.A.), and Ph.D. programs across 12 departments, focusing on engineering, technology, management, and computer applications. Accredited with an NAAC A++ grade (CGPA 3.62/4.00) in 2022 and NBA accreditation for eligible undergraduate programs, KCT is ranked in the 151-200 band in the National Institutional Ranking Framework (NIRF) Engineering category as of 2025. KCT emphasizes innovation, research, and industry collaboration, with notable achievements including 71 patents filed, over 30,000 alumni worldwide, and more than 1,300 annual scholarships awarded to students. The institution features advanced infrastructure such as 165 laboratories and 10 government- or industry-sponsored centers of excellence, alongside 13 Memoranda of Understanding (MoUs) with international universities to foster global competencies. Its placement record is robust, with 616 recruiters participating in 2024 (as of November 2025), including approximately 326 core engineering companies (53%), 166 product-based firms (27%), 68 IT service-based organizations (11%), and 55 management consultancies (9%), preparing students for technological, economic, and social contributions. Founded with a vision to become a technical university of international standards, KCT upholds a mission of providing quality education through continuous improvement and holistic development. # Kumaraguru College of Technology
## Academics
### Programs Offered
Kumaraguru College of Technology offers a range of undergraduate programs in engineering and technology, spanning 14 disciplines as of 2025. These four-year Bachelor of Engineering (B.E.) and Bachelor of Technology (B.Tech.) degrees each require 120 credits and emphasize practical skills alongside theoretical foundations. The programs include B.E. in Aeronautical Engineering, Automobile Engineering, Civil Engineering, Computer Science and Engineering, Electrical and Electronics Engineering, Electronics and Communication Engineering, Electronics and Instrumentation Engineering, Fashion Technology, Information Technology, Mechanical Engineering, Mechatronics Engineering, and Textile Technology; and B.Tech. in Artificial Intelligence and Data Science, and Biotechnology.
The institution provides 19 postgraduate programs as of 2025, primarily two-year Master of Engineering (M.E.) and Master of Technology (M.Tech.) degrees, along with Master of Computer Applications (MCA) and Master of Business Administration (MBA). These programs focus on advanced specializations, including emerging technologies such as artificial intelligence, VLSI design, and data sciences. Key offerings encompass M.E. in Applied Electronics, CAD/CAM, Communication Systems, Computer Science and Engineering, Construction Engineering and Management, Control and Instrumentation, Embedded Systems, Energy Engineering, Engineering Design, Environmental Engineering, Industrial Engineering, Structural Engineering, and VLSI Design; M.Tech. in Apparel Technology and Biotechnology; MCA; and MBA.
Doctoral programs at the college include Ph.D. degrees offered across 12 departments, such as Computer Science and Engineering, Electronics and Communication Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, Civil Engineering, Biotechnology, and others. Research emphases include sustainable engineering practices and data analytics, with both full-time and part-time options available to support scholarly inquiry in these fields.
Admissions to undergraduate programs are facilitated through the Tamil Nadu Engineering Admissions (TNEA) process, based on Class 12 merit. Postgraduate admissions rely on scores from the Graduate Aptitude Test in Engineering (GATE) or Tamil Nadu Common Entrance Test (TANCET), followed by counseling. For instance, computer science-related branches (CSE, IT, AI&DS) collectively accommodate around 350 seats. The college's autonomous status enables curriculum flexibility to integrate industry-relevant updates across these programs

In 2025, out of 981 students who registered for campus placements, 832 has been successfully placed, resulting in an 85% placement rate. A total of 982 job offers were made, which means some students received more than one offer. The top recruiting sectors for this batch were Core engineering (233 students) and Product-based companies (155 students), followed by IT services and management roles.

The 2024 batch did slightly better, achieving a 90% placement rate. Out of 1009 registered students, 911 got placed, with a total of 1364 offers, showing that many students received multiple job offers. The 2023 and 2022 batches had exceptional placement rates of 97%. In 2023, 1010 students out of 1042 were placed, with 1532 total offers. In 2022, 993 students out of 1021 were placed. This consistent performance shows that KCT Coimbatore has maintained a strong industry connection and provides excellent support to students in securing jobs in diverse fields such as Core Engineering, Product Development, IT Services, and Management.

**Placement Percentage:**

* In 2024, the placement rate was 90%, with 911 out of 1009 registered students placed.

* In 2025, the placement rate slightly dipped to 85%, with 832 out of 981 students placed.

**Job Offers Trend:**

* The number of total offers decreased from 1364 in 2024 to 982 in 2025, indicating a drop in offer volume.

**Sector-Wise Placement (2024-2025 batch):**

* The majority of students in 2024–2025 were placed in the Core (233) and Product (155) sectors.

* Fewer students went into IT Services (44) and Management (29), suggesting a continued strong focus on core and product-based roles.

## About Kumaraguru College of Technology, Coimbatore
Kumaraguru College of Technology Coimbatore also known as KCT Coimbatore is a private institution that was established in 1984 under the guidance of the Ramanandha Adigalar Foundation. Kumaraguru College of Technology has been approved by the All Indian Council for Technical Education (AICTE) and is also affiliated to Anna University.

* **KCT Coimbatore** is ranked in the band of 151-200 in the Engineering category as per NIRF ranking 2025.
* Courses at **KCT Coimbatore **offered are B.Tech, M.Tech, MCA, and PhD.
* The entrance exams required for admissions at **KCT Coimbatore **are TNEA/ TANCET/ GATE.
* **KCT Coimbatore fees**: for B.Tech is Rs 4.03 lakhs; for MCA is Rs 2.66 lakhs.
* **Kumaraguru College of Technology**** seat intake**: for B.Tech is 1488; for M.Tech is 126; for MCA is 60.
* According to the NIRF 2024 data, the **KCT Coimbatore median package** offered for the UG course is Rs 5 lakhs, and a total of 949 students got placed out of 1229 graduating students.

Meanwhile, KCT Coimbatore offers graduate courses that lead to M.E/MTech, and MCA degrees, with fourteen specialisation programmes. Eleven research specialisations are available in PhD curricula offering KCT doctoral courses.

# Kumaraguru College of Technology, Coimbatore Facilities Details: Hostel, Campus, Infrastructure, Library, Canteen
## Kumaraguru College of Technology, Coimbatore Facilities
Kumaraguru College of Technology (KCT) provides many facilities to the students and faculty such as separate hostels for boys and girls, sports facilities so that students can take a break from their busy schedule, library with a good collection of books.

**Also See:** KCT Coimbatore cutoffs

It also provides facilities like an auditorium for seminars/conferences, computing facilities, a cafeteria, and medical assistance for emergency dhyan mandapam. Other facilities like banking, internet, etc are also available for them. The details of KCT facilities are mentioned below:

* Boys Hostel

There are separate hostels for boys within the KCT campus. The boys’ hostel has 510 rooms that can accommodate around 2088 students. There is a common area facility like dining area, lobby, washrooms, etc.

* Girls Hostel

There are separate hostels for girls within the KCT campus. The girls’ hostel has 264 rooms that can accommodate 1216 students. There is a common area facility like dining area, lobby, washrooms, etc.

* Medical/Hospital

There is an eight bedded hospital (Aruljothi Medical Centre) in the KCT campus with 2 doctors and nursing staff.

* Gym

There are separate gyms for men and women students and staff laid out in an area of 2400 sq. ft. with the latest equipment.

* Library

The central library in the KCT campus owns 107534 volumes and 67598 titles and has a subscription to 8829 online international journals, 186 print journals and 59 magazines. It provides access to e-resources and printing.

* Sports

The 23 acres playfield in the campus is meant for outdoor games and sports such as hockey, football, cricket, badminton, volleyball, tennis, handball, throwball, etc.

* I.T Infrastructure

A large number of personal computers cater to the academic needs of all the departments on the campus. 256 Mbps internet connectivity is available in the campus from Airtel internet service provider. There is a CAD lab with 120 PCs used by departments involved in designing structures, vehicles, equipment, etc.

* Cafeteria

A spacious and trendy cafeteria in the campus offers delicious food and snacks in a hygienic environment to students and staff. High-quality vegetarian food is prepared in the cafeteria. Bakery items and fresh fruit juices are also available in the cafeteria. Canteen timings are 7:30 am to 5:30 pm on working days.

* Auditorium

The KCT campus has an air-conditioned auditorium with a capacity of 1800 seating used for all events and functions.

* Transport Facility

KCT has a number of buses, cars and vans for the conveyance of students and staff. The buses operate at different routes around Coimbatore starting between 6:30 and 6:45 am.

* Alumni Associations

KCT Alumni Guest Lecture is a series of free lectures and talks by featuring speakers, often past KCT alumni, who are excellence in Technology, Engineering, Management and Entrepreneurship. You can expect thought-provoking discussions, engaging topics and networking with other KCT alumni over light refreshments. We invite you to join us and experience your alumni community. Alumni Guest Lectures brings KCT Alumni to the College Campus to discuss recent breakthroughs and discoveries, offering alumni and friends a chance to share ideas and conversation.
